title: tylophora ridleyi
intro: Tylophora ridleyi, also known as the Indian ipecacuanha or snake root, is a plant species native to the Western Ghats of India. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cough, and acting as an antiseptic agent to prevent infections. Tylophora ridleyi is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be taken internally or applied topically for its medicinal effects.
